title: Use of There
description: Learn to use 'There is', 'There are', 'There was', 'There were', and 'There will be' to describe existence and presence of things and people. One of the most common sentence structures in English.

subtopics.title: There Is — Singular
subtopics.description: There is a book. There is a problem. Singular noun after there is.

subtopics.title: There Are — Plural
subtopics.description: There are many students. There are two chairs. Plural nouns after there are.

subtopics.title: There Was / There Were — Past
subtopics.description: There was a tree here. There were many people. Past tense of there is/are.

subtopics.title: There Will Be — Future
subtopics.description: There will be a meeting. There will be many guests. Future tense.

subtopics.title: Questions with There
subtopics.description: Is there a doctor? Are there any seats? Were there many people?

subtopics.title: Negatives with There
subtopics.description: There is no milk. There are no chairs. There was no one there.

learningObjectives: Use there is with singular nouns
Use there are with plural nouns
Use there was and there were in the past
Use there will be for future statements
Form questions using Is there / Are there
Form negatives using there is no / there are no
Use there in real daily conversations
